questions: Should this cleanup rename the public/editor callback surface to `onClipEdgeResizeEnd`, or should the unification stay internal and keep `onActionResizeEnd` at the component/context boundary as a compatibility layer?

For outer-group preview, should the synthetic preview key `${shotId}:${rowId}` remain as an internal TimelineCanvas detail for [positionedShotGroups](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx#L582), or do you want that preview derived exclusively from returned clip updates with no separate group override entry?

Do you want outer-edge resizes to start using the same `onActionResizeStart` bookkeeping path as free/interior resizes, or should external start-callback behavior remain unchanged while only update/end/commit become unified?

success_criteria.criterion: `applyClipEdgeMove` exists in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.ts` and unit tests cover `free`, `interior`, and `outer` contexts, including clamp behavior.

`resolveClipEdgeResizeContext` exists and `TimelineCanvas` pointer-down resolves one unified `ClipEdgeResizeSession` instead of constructing separate `clip` / `group-child-boundary` / `group-edge` session types.
`updateResize` and `endResize` in `TimelineCanvas.tsx` no longer branch on three session kinds; commit/cancel behavior flows through one unified path.
`startGroupEdgeResize` is removed from `useClipResize.ts` and from the timeline editor/context/provider wiring surface.
Grepping the resize lifecycle/wiring files returns zero hits for `kind: 'group-child-boundary'`, `kind: 'group-edge'`, `startGroupEdgeResize`, and `[resize-diag]`.
The existing grouped/off-center resize integration tests in `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` still pass, and at least one new end-to-end free-clip resize test is added.
The video-editor test suite passes after the refactor, with the resize-related test count increased by at least one over the current baseline.
`tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` completes cleanly for the video-editor changes.
Existing in-suite regressions outside resize lifecycle remain green, including grouped drag, migration load, final-video switching UI, auto-save/save-path tests, and cursor-offset tracking coverage.
Net line count across the resize lifecycle code decreases versus the current baseline in `TimelineCanvas.tsx`, `useClipResize.ts`, and `resize-math.ts`.
Public prop/type churn is limited to the resize pathway; unrelated drag, persistence, and migration code stays untouched.
Manual symptom check: the 'boundary doesn't track cursor during drag' issue is no longer reproducible through the unified path.

assumptions: Follow the brief’s architecture: one session type, one dispatcher, one commit path; do not reimplement resize math already covered by `computeGroupChildBoundaryResize`, `computeGroupEdgeResize`, or `computeClipResizeMetaPatch`.

Keep the refactor scoped to resize lifecycle files: `TimelineCanvas.tsx`, `useClipResize.ts`, `resize-math.ts`, their tests, and only the minimal state/context/provider wiring required to remove dead resize APIs.
Preserve current data shapes, persistence behavior, drag behavior, and migration/save flows; any changes in those areas would be treated as regressions, not acceptable cleanup.
If the public callback rename is not explicitly approved, prefer a compatibility-preserving boundary and unify the internals first.

Keep the synthetic group preview override internal to `TimelineCanvas` unless the callback/API decision explicitly requires removing it; `applyClipEdgeMove` should remain clip-update-oriented as specified.
Use the current minimum-duration constants and existing snap behavior as the behavioral baseline; this plan changes structure, not product rules.
